entries.title: Science: Lithium, Law of Conservation of Mass, and Lysosome
entries.content: In the field of Chemistry and cellular biology in Switzerland, research studies how the chemical element Lithium (Li, atomic number 3, classified as a alkali metal; lightest solid metal, highly reactive, used in rechargeable batteries) interacts under conditions governed by Law of Conservation of Mass (principle: mass in an isolated system is neither created nor destroyed by chemical reactions; formula: Mass of reactants = Mass of products) inside the Lysosome (contains digestive enzymes to break down waste materials and cellular debris).

entries.title: History Analysis: Russian Revolution and Qin Shi Huang
entries.content: The historical event of the Russian Revolution (1917 in Russia), which abolished the monarchy and led to the creation of the Soviet Union, is often compared to the legacy of Qin Shi Huang in Imperial China (unifying China and starting Great Wall construction) in the realm of global trade routes in France.
